title: Repeated full-corpus loading of event ledger into memory
description: readEventLedger reads and JSON-parses the entire events.jsonl file into memory. It is called repeatedly (8+ times) across inspection functions like inspectEventLedger, inspectCiteGoodhart, buildDriftedStableIds, and inspectDriftUnconsumed. For large event ledgers this results in unbounded memory allocation and CPU overhead.
fix_direction: Parse the ledger once per doctor run and pass the resulting events array to all dependent inspection functions, or use a stream parser.

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Race condition in event ledger truncation
description: truncateLedgerToLastNewline runs without a cross-process lock. If an active client session appends an event concurrently while doctor is truncating the ledger to fix a partial write, the concurrent write will be destroyed or cause further file corruption.
fix_direction: Implement a cross-process lock (e.g., .fabric/events.jsonl.lock) that agents must acquire before appending and doctor must acquire before truncating.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: God-file: doctor.ts (8697 lines)
description: Massive 8697-line dependency hub handling almost all server linting and fixing logic, coupled to nearly every service.
fix_direction: Decompose into smaller, domain-specific lint and repair services.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Interactive elements lack semantic tags — not keyboard accessible
description: Sidebar registry tree items use non-interactive <div> with cursor-pointer, missing tabindex, ARIA role (role=button/treeitem) and keyboard listeners. Screen readers cannot identify them and keyboard-only users cannot navigate or activate the nodes.
fix_direction: Replace interactive <div> with <button>/<a>; if <div> is required, add tabindex=0, the appropriate role, and Enter/Space key handling.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Personal-layer knowledge (KP-*) can leak into team read-set
description: Personal-layer (KP-*) and team-layer (KT-*) knowledge nodes are built together and stored equally in agents.meta.json. If downstream recall (fab_recall / read-sets) does not enforce required_stores isolation for personal, personal knowledge and intent data may leak into shared team context.
fix_direction: Enforce required_stores authorization at the knowledge-graph read layer (recall/query), isolating the personal tree by default.

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Synchronous file system reads in full-corpus scans (iterateCanonicalEntries)
description: iterateCanonicalEntries uses readdirSync and readFileSync. It is called multiple times sequentially across inspections (inspectDraftAutoPromote, inspectDraftBacklog, inspectKnowledgeTagsEmpty). This causes N+1 blocking sync I/O calls scaling linearly with knowledge entries, blocking the event loop on hot paths.
fix_direction: Replace sync FS calls with async variants (fs/promises) or cache the canonical entries map across the doctor run.

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Race condition during agents.meta.json counter repairs
description: fixCounterDesync and applyIndexDriftFix perform a read-modify-write on agents.meta.json to update knowledge ID counters. If another client concurrently allocates a new KB ID (bumping the counter), doctor will overwrite the meta file with a stale, lower counter value, leading to subsequent stable_id collisions.
fix_direction: Apply optimistic concurrency control (check revision/hash before writing) or a cross-process file lock during meta repairs.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Non-atomic multi-step meta updates
description: In the reconcile block, doctor calls reconcileKnowledge followed by fixCounterDesync. Both perform independent atomic writes to agents.meta.json. Concurrent readers can observe the intermediate state where nodes are reconciled but counters are not, leading to invalid ID allocations.
fix_direction: Merge node reconciliation and counter desync repairs into a single read-modify-write atomic transaction on agents.meta.json.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: God-file: install.ts
description: Overlong function and file handling complex installation logic, multi-client support, and interactive prompts.
fix_direction: Move execution logic to separate services/classes and abstract prompt handling.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Silent Event Ledger append failure (no visibility to human or AI)
description: In runDoctorFix and runDoctorApplyLint, event ledger appends (bootstrap_marker_migrated, serve_lock_cleared, etc.) use .catch(() => {}). On disk-full/permission failure the file fix still applies but the audit event is lost, desyncing metadata from actual state with zero error output, so neither the human dev nor the AI agent can perceive the failure.
fix_direction: Do not silently swallow; capture non-fatal ledger append failures as a warning / failed-mutation entry in the returned DoctorFixReport/DoctorApplyLintReport so callers see the desync.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Search input removes keyboard focus indicator
description: Top search input hardcodes outline-none with no compensating focus state (e.g. focus:ring), so keyboard users tabbing through cannot see the current focus position.
fix_direction: Remove outline-none or add a WCAG-compliant focus outline via focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-accent.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Insufficient text contrast in light mode
description: On light backgrounds, text-brand-accent (#3b82f6) is ~3.1:1 and text-brand-warning (#f59e0b) is ~2.1:1, both below WCAG AA 4.5:1 for small text.
fix_direction: Map higher-contrast colors (700 variants) in the light-mode Tailwind theme or darken weight/palette to reach at least 4.5:1.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Sensitive data / PII written to disk without fine-grained redaction
description: extract-knowledge.ts hasSecrets is only a hard-block gate; free text that passes (PII or unrecognized secrets) is written via atomicWriteText with no content-level redaction. event-ledger.ts redactSecretsInEvent only redacts by predefined field-name whitelist, not deep-cleaning all payload fields, so sensitive data may persist into the KB or event ledger.
fix_direction: Introduce content-based PII/secrets scanning + redaction before knowledge is persisted, and extend redactSecretsInEvent to cover dynamic payload content.

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Unbounded memory growth in readEventLedger / rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded
description: Both functions load the entire events.jsonl into memory via readFile and split by newline. For a 50MB ledger (the soft-warning threshold) this instantiates millions of strings simultaneously, causing heavy GC pressure and potential OOM under concurrent sessions or large corpora.
fix_direction: Migrate readEventLedger and rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded to readline.createInterface + fs.createReadStream for O(1)-memory linear parsing.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: O(N) full-corpus in-memory file scans during search
description: searchEntries performs a linear O(N) scan across all pending and canonical trees, calling readFile on every markdown file to parse frontmatter for titles/summaries/tags. This O(N) FS + parsing load degrades search latency severely as the KB grows 10x-100x.
fix_direction: Implement a pre-computed description_index or SQLite backing store to serve search queries without blocking on thousands of file reads per request.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Sequential asynchronous file system writes during lint application
description: In runDoctorApplyLint, file mutations (applyOrphanDemote, applyStaleArchive, applyPendingAutoArchive) are performed sequentially in for...of loops instead of concurrently. This linearly increases execution time on large repositories with many auto-fixable findings.
fix_direction: Use Promise.all to process independent file mutations concurrently within each lint pass, or batch the I/O operations.

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Synchronous I/O in hook inspections
description: inspectHooksRuntime, inspectHooksContentDrift, and inspectHooksWired use synchronous methods (readFileSync, readdirSync, statSync) to inspect hook files, blocking the event loop.
fix_direction: Refactor hook inspection functions to use async fs/promises and evaluate them concurrently within the initial Promise.all block.

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Silent fallback on invalid configuration
description: resolvePayloadLimits wraps readPayloadLimits in a try/catch and silently falls back to library defaults on failure. If a user introduces a syntax error in fabric.config.json, doctor will silently use defaults without surfacing the parsing error.
fix_direction: Surface configuration read/parse errors as a warning or info in the doctor report instead of silently swallowing the exception.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Duplicated Logic: Configuration Loading
description: Server reads config from root 'fabric.config.json' while CLI looks for '.fabric/fabric-config.json', duplicating read/validation logic.
fix_direction: Centralize configuration I/O and validation in the shared package.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Inconsistent Contracts: Project Root Resolution
description: Duplicated path resolution logic across packages (resolveProjectRoot, ProjectRootResolver, normalizeProjectRoot).
fix_direction: Standardize on a single project root resolution strategy in shared package.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Ambiguous 'triage by hand' hint is not actionable for AI consumer
description: On stable_id_duplicate / layer_mismatch (data-overwrite risk) apply-lint aborts with abort_reason 'triage by hand'. An MCP AI consumer cannot tell which concrete operations to perform (which file to delete, which frontmatter ID to change).
fix_direction: Provide precise machine-readable guidance in abort_reason / actionHint: list conflicting absolute file paths and which MCP tools resolve the conflict.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Cognitive friction from --fix vs --apply-lint split
description: Workspace health repair is hard-split into runDoctorFix (ledger rotation, meta alignment, dir repair) and runDoctorApplyLint (frontmatter state changes). The two-headed interface confuses users; a human or AI may run --fix and miss the substantive lint correction.
fix_direction: Unify both flows under one entry, or have runDoctorFix's report detect remaining lint findings and prominently prompt the next-step doctor --apply-lint in CLI and MCP return values.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Key shortcut hint hidden from screen readers
description: The ⌘K shortcut hint next to search has aria-hidden=true with no sr-only alternative, so screen-reader users cannot learn the shortcut.
fix_direction: Include the shortcut in the input aria-label (e.g. aria-label="Search path (Command K)") or remove aria-hidden and provide sr-only readable text.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Continuous pulse animation lacks reduced-motion support
description: The 'MCP Connected' status dot uses animate-pulse (infinite loop) with no prefers-reduced-motion handling, which can cause discomfort for vestibular-disorder users.
fix_direction: Enable motion-safe/reduce variants globally in Tailwind config or add motion-reduce:animate-none on the element.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Audit trail gap: agents.meta.json repair skips event emission
description: When apply-lint handles knowledge_index_drift it updates counters in agents.meta.json via atomicWriteJson but intentionally skips event recording (comment: 'NO event emission — the schema does not (yet) carry an agents_meta_repaired event type'). This state change bypasses the ledger, breaking the Event Ledger as the single audit trail.
fix_direction: Add a dedicated audit event type (e.g. agents_meta_repaired) so all meta state mutations are recorded in the ledger.

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Linear directory scans for session hint hygiene
description: Session hints create per-session cache files (.fabric/.cache/session-hints-*.json). inspectSessionHintsStale scans the cache directory linearly to purge stale sessions. With concurrent multi-window sessions on the same repo these files accrete rapidly, making readdirSync progressively slower.
fix_direction: Consolidate session hint state into a unified LRU cache file or lightweight store, or shard by day to bound directory listings.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Hardcoded directory structures impede multi-store scaling
description: Multiple services (review, extract-knowledge, doctor lints) hardcode iterations over static paths like .fabric/knowledge and ~/.fabric/knowledge. As the multi-store git model (~/.fabric/stores/<id>) expands, these static loops fail to discover cross-store knowledge dynamically or incur heavy latency enumerating cloned stores.
fix_direction: Abstract knowledge paths into a dynamic store registry/workspace locator that resolves ~/.fabric/stores/<id> and queries only relevant indexed stores.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Duplicated Logic: Frontmatter Parsing
description: Complex frontmatter extraction and parsing overlaps between knowledge-meta-builder and _shared extractBody.
fix_direction: Unify frontmatter and body extraction logic in the shared package.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Leaky Abstraction: Error Duck-typing
description: CLI uses duck-typing (FabricErrorShape) for FabricError due to class identity issues across package boundaries.
fix_direction: Enhance the shared error model so cross-package boundaries retain class identity.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Hardcoded hygiene thresholds not configurable
description: NARROW_RATIO_THRESHOLD (0.2), SILENCE_RATE_THRESHOLD (0.95), SESSION_HINTS_STALE_DAYS (7) are hardcoded. Different AI model context windows make hardcoded thresholds cause false positives that users cannot silence via config.
fix_direction: Follow payload_limits / underseed_node_threshold: move these constants into the fabric.config.json override layer in config-loader.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Lock-file cleanup throws uncaught FS error, aborting whole fix flow
description: When clearing a stale .fabric/.serve.lock, a non-ENOENT error (e.g. EACCES) is thrown directly, crashing the entire long runDoctorFix flow at its tail and failing other otherwise-recoverable repair steps.
fix_direction: Catch non-ENOENT errors without throwing; record as manual_error in the report so the fix flow degrades gracefully.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Redundant ARIA labels on navigation links
description: Sidebar nav <a> tags (Readiness, Rules Explain) already have visible text but hardcode identical aria-label, causing some screen readers to announce link text twice.
fix_direction: Remove the redundant aria-label since the links already have visible readable text.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Archived data lacks retention/rotation policy — unbounded growth
description: Stale knowledge nodes (stale_archive) and over-age pending proposals (pending_auto_archive) are only fs.renamed into .fabric/.archive/, and the event ledger is only rotated; there is no TTL or MaxBytes destruction/rotation policy for the .archive tombstone directory, so storage grows unbounded over time.
fix_direction: Add a global archive eviction mechanism with configurable max archive size or retention days and periodic physical deletion of over-age files.

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)

title: Hardcoded default top_k silently truncates matches
description: The retrieval pipeline ranks candidates and truncates with a default plan_context_top_k limit. As corpus size scales 10x-100x, highly relevant entries are silently omitted if they rank below the static count threshold, causing retrieval starvation on large KBs.
fix_direction: Adopt a dynamic top_k that scales with corpus size, or rely on a BM25/vector score minimum threshold instead of an absolute scalar cap.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-100005 (Gemini delegate)
